moves.title: Invariant-call route
moves.applicability: This is a `call (_: Inv)` route template, not a complete tactic; fill in the invariant expression from the current goal before probing or committing it.
moves.runnable_status: Not yet; replace `Inv` with a concrete EasyCrypt invariant before probing or committing this tactic.
moves.needs_frame: Postcondition shape only — no `={...}` equality frame. Build the `={...}` part from `application_context.write_map` (per candidate field: who writes it — the read-only/preserved fields are safe to equate), then add your coupling before this is a usable invariant.
how_to_complete: Read the current precondition, postcondition, and the two visible call sites; include the state that must be preserved across the adversary or oracle call.
If the call generates oracle obligations, include the global/oracle state needed by the named oracle-equivalence handles visible in the current context.

inspect_if_unsure.why: Shows the live call frontier and any named call or oracle-equivalence handles.
After you have a candidate invariant, previews the obligations and missing facts before changing the proof state.

limitations: The invariant is not filled in yet; use the current goal and, if helpful, call-subgoal preview to choose the concrete invariant.

moves.title: Named-call context
moves.applicability: This named-call shape came from program comparison. Treat it as context until a manager inspection or read-only probe confirms the lemma matches the visible call frontier.
moves.runnable_status: Not established as runnable from this panel; use the current frontier and call-site context before deciding whether to probe it.
missing_input: Concrete arguments required by the lemma or call form.
how_to_complete: Match the lemma or call statement against the visible call sites, module parameters, globals, and hypotheses in the current goal.
If the arity or argument order is unclear, inspect the tactic form or lookup the symbol before guessing.

Do not treat this as the next call tactic solely because the name is visible; the current frontier still determines whether it applies.

ask_manager_for.use_when: Need parsed goal shape, resolved names, or KB pattern hints.
Current Pr equality may need a manager-verified bridge route (game-hop or scheme/endpoint normalization) before direct byequiv/proc lowering.

items.label: Before inline expansion #2
items.committed_tactic: have -> : Pr[CCA_game(A, RealOrcls(ChaChaPoly)).main() @ &m : res] = Pr[CCA_game(A, RealOrcls(OpCCinit.OChaChaPoly(I_stateless))).main() @ &m : res] by byequiv=>//; proc; inline *; sim.
items.why_checkpoint: inline boundary; selecting it restores the proof state before this expansion
items.effect_if_selected: Undo committed tactic #2 and every committed tactic after it in this node.
